extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian used ROGLIC's slipstream on the final Mur de Huy ascent, sat on VALVERDE's attack, then timed his acceleration to perfection for his third win.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC went early on final Mur ascent, forcing tempo but leaving him vulnerable to slipstream exploitation by followers.
ALAPHILIPPE refused to panic when VALVERDE moved alongside, demonstrating superior race craft by delaying final acceleration.
CARAPAZ attacked on Cote de Cherave as decoy/setup for PIDCOCK or YATES rather than personal victory attempt.
VALVERDE used pavement to reposition before final climb, showing positioning desperation typically punished but here yielded podium.

rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: negative: crashed and required GEOGHEGAN HART relay back, yet still finished sixth showing remarkable recovery capacity
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: negative: bridged to break on Cote de Cherave but move immediately neutralized showing poor timing judgment
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: forced to lead out final climb suggesting tactical isolation or misread of Alaphilippe's positioning threat
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: fourth place finish demonstrates Mur de Huy suitability beyond typical palmares expectations
rider_signals.UAE Team Emirates: negative: entire team DNS due to COVID protocol revealing organizational vulnerability
